title: NeuronMessageEnvelope
description: Versioned multi-part message envelope returned by the Neuron companion (F0038-S0002, ADR-027 §6). A message carries an ordered list of typed parts (text | app | status | sources | actions) and is keyed by thread_id so persisted threads replay correctly as the app-part schema evolves. The frontend renders only registered component identifiers with validated props (component registry, intake L1) — Neuron never returns executable markup or model-emitted numbers. Maps A2A messages/parts/artifacts onto these part types.

envelope_version.description: Envelope schema version. Additive evolution only without a major bump; removing or retyping a part shape requires a bump so persisted threads replay deterministically.

thread_id.description: Conversation thread that keys all companion state (intake seam 1). Maps to A2A contextId. Single-thread v1 ('Day at a Glance' / 'Renewals'); thread management UX is F0039.

message_id.description: Stable id for this message; corresponds to a neuron.messages row.

in_reply_to_message_id.description: Optional id of the message this one responds to.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset or 'Z'.

parts.description: Ordered parts assembled into the rendered message.

text.description: Plain text; never interpreted as markup.

component.description: Registered component identifier. The frontend rejects any identifier not in the component registry and renders a safe fallback (F0038-S0002).

props.description: Props validated against the registered component's prop schema before render. No executable markup.

state.description: A2A-task-state-aligned status surfaced to the user.

ref.description: Internal reference id (e.g. renewal id); never a raw external URL with PII.

action_id.description: Registered action identifier echoed back to the action-callback endpoint.

action_type.description: Registered, allow-listed action. The callback handler re-authorizes server-side; the action payload never carries authorization claims.

payload.description: Action-specific data (e.g. renewalId) carried back on invocation.
